name: Refactor and Update Tests

tasks.title: Refactor atomicWriteJSON to delegate to AtomicWriteFile
tasks.description: Refactor atomicWriteJSON to delegate to AtomicWriteFile
tasks.body:
Replace the duplicated temp-file-rename logic in atomicWriteJSON (lines 107-143 of internal/state/io.go) with a three-line body:

1. json.MarshalIndent(v, "", "  ")
2. append trailing newline
3. return AtomicWriteFile(path, data)

Remove the stale comment on line 70 ("// atomicWriteFile writes raw bytes...") which duplicates the godoc on line 71-73.

Error wrapping will change: callers that currently see "creating state directory" will now see "creating directory" (from AtomicWriteFile). This is fine; the next task updates affected tests.
acceptance_criteria: atomicWriteJSON calls json.MarshalIndent then AtomicWriteFile and nothing else
The trailing newline append is preserved before delegating
The old comment on line 70 (duplicate of line 71) is removed

tasks.title: Update tests for changed error messages
tasks.description: Update tests for changed error messages
tasks.body:
After the refactor, atomicWriteJSON delegates to AtomicWriteFile, so its error paths now wrap through AtomicWriteFile's error messages rather than its own. Review every test file that exercises atomicWriteJSON error paths:

- internal/state/io_chmod_test.go (lines 32, 56, 75 reference atomicWriteJSON error strings)
- internal/state/io_coverage_test.go (lines 30, 46, 61-70, 82)
- internal/state/io_deep_coverage_test.go
- internal/state/io_write_error_test.go

Update any hardcoded error-message assertions to match the new wrapping. For example, "creating state directory" becomes "creating directory". Run the full test suite to confirm.
acceptance_criteria: go test ./internal/state/... passes with no failures
No test assertions reference error strings that only existed in the old atomicWriteJSON implementation (e.g. creating state directory)

tasks.title: Audit
tasks.description: Verify all work in Refactor and Update Tests is complete and correct

audit_enrichment: Confirm atomicWriteJSON body is exactly: marshal, append newline, delegate to AtomicWriteFile. No leftover temp-file logic.
Confirm go test ./internal/state/... passes with zero failures
Grep test files for 'creating state directory' to ensure no stale error assertions remain

breadcrumbs.text: Refactored atomicWriteJSON to a 3-line delegate: MarshalIndent, append newline, AtomicWriteFile. Removed stale duplicate comment. Build and tests pass.

Reviewed all test files for error-string assertions after atomicWriteJSON refactor. No tests matched on specific error message text; all use behavioral err == nil / err != nil checks. No changes needed. go test ./internal/state/... passes.

Audit reviewed io.go diff (30 lines removed, 3-line delegate body added), all 4 test files, and 2 production callers (inbox_io.go, review_io.go). Verified: build passes, tests pass with -race, go vet clean, gofmt clean. No error-string assertions exist anywhere in the test suite. Old 'creating state directory' string gone from all .go files. No dead code, no unused imports, no regressions. PASS with zero findings.

audit.result_summary: Refactored atomicWriteJSON from a 40-line function with its own temp-file-rename implementation to a 3-line delegate that marshals JSON and calls AtomicWriteFile. Removed a stale comment that duplicated the AtomicWriteFile godoc. All existing tests pass without modification because they use behavioral assertions (err nil/non-nil) rather than error-string matching. Net result: 30 lines of duplicated logic eliminated with zero behavioral change at any call site.

task-0001.objective: Remove duplicated atomic-write logic from atomicWriteJSON by delegating to AtomicWriteFile
task-0001.what_happened: Replaced the 30-line temp-file-rename body in atomicWriteJSON with three lines: MarshalIndent, append newline, return AtomicWriteFile. Removed the stale comment on what was line 70 that duplicated the godoc for AtomicWriteFile.
went_well: Net deletion of 30 lines with zero behavioral change at the caller level
improvements: Error message wording shifts from 'creating state directory' to 'creating directory'; the next task (task-0002) is expected to update affected test assertions
action_items: task-0002 should update any test assertions that match on 'creating state directory'

task-0002.objective: Update test assertions that match on old atomicWriteJSON error strings
task-0002.what_happened: Reviewed all four deliverable test files (io_chmod_test.go, io_coverage_test.go, io_deep_coverage_test.go, io_write_error_test.go) and grepped the entire state package for error string assertions. No tests match on specific error message text; all error-path tests use behavioral assertions (err == nil / err \!= nil). The anticipated 'creating state directory' string match does not exist in any test. Full test suite passes.
went_well: Tests were already written with resilient behavioral assertions rather than brittle string matching, so the refactor required zero test changes
improvements: None needed; the test design was already correct
action_items: None; all acceptance criteria are met
